meta.title: About Me | Pavel Kalandra — kalandra.tech
meta.description: About Pavel Kalandra — career journey from C# developer to lead engineer.

hero.name: Pavel Kalandra
hero.subtitle: Software engineer and engineering leader dedicated to building resilient systems and high-performing teams.

personal.title: About me
personal.subtitle: A few things I get up to when I'm away from the keyboard.

items.title: Games of every kind

items.description: I used to spend a lot of time doing sports. Now I need to pace myself due to ankle injuries.

items.title: Father of two
items.description: Most of my non-work time goes here.

pillars.title: Focus on your product, save on maintenance
pillars.description: Every system you ship is a system you keep forever. Build simple when possible, go complex when necessary. Microservices aren't a default — they're either a must-have or overkill.

pillars.title: Transparency and honesty
pillars.description: Hard decisions need full information. What teams don't say openly, they gossip about privately.

pillars.title: A Teamplayer
pillars.description: Show up every day with the intent to improve something — code, tests, a teammate's day. Rely on others, and be reliable for others.

pillars.title: Empowered teams
pillars.description: Autonomous cultures where every engineer has end-to-end ownership and room to leave a mark.
paragraphs: Align team structure with product structure so engineers own a domain end-to-end, not just the backlog. Accountability and responsibility go hand in hand. That's empowerment.
Give the team a clear vision, budget time for bugs and escalations, and leave 10–20% unbooked so engineers can chase their own ideas. Solving problems is fun; being a coding monkey with no say isn't.

skills.label: Toolbox
skills.title: Skills

items.title: Continuous discovery

items.title: The "why" behind the "what"
items.description: Interrogating feature requests for the underlying job-to-be-done.

items.title: User disengagement
items.description: The best UX is the one the user never has to open. Surface anomalies, free the human.

entries.description:
A hands-on role leading a single-pizza team building an app for selling tickets from third-party providers.

Owning the overall system architecture, feature design, backlog prioritization and engineering ways of working — while still shipping production code every day.
Didn't stay long, left for a great opportunity as Lead Engineer at tickadoo.

My only hands-off role — middle management. Led roughly one third of engineering — a team of 15.

I ran an initiative to align team structure with product structure so engineers could have end-to-end ownership of their domain. I put principles and processes in place that kept technical quality from being sacrificed under tight deadlines. I built a transparent roadmap to manage stakeholder delivery expectations and discuss staffing requirements. I hired and coached new leaders.

subEntries.description: Single-handedly drove a DX initiative that cut local startup time by 61%, saved 137 GB of database bloat, and overhauled the FuncSharp library — improving hot-path performance and adding stronger type-safety to prevent entire categories of bugs at compile time. Championed culture changes across backend engineering.
Led cross-functional team overseeing billing, reporting, and fiscal compliance. Transitioned to multi-tax systems for global expansion.

entries.description: Owned an ASP.NET web application end-to-end, including the Azure infrastructure it ran on — Azure SQL, Azure Table Storage, and Azure Active Directory. Replaced the legacy SSRS reports with an interactive Vue.js frontend that pulled data from a dedicated API and rendered responsive d3.js charts, complete with a sidebar filter so users could slice the data themselves.
Started as a C# intern during university, progressing through WPF development and CodedUI testing. Built desktop applications and tablet interfaces.
